metadata.title: Existence and Uniqueness of the Trawin Zero Point
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Existence and Uniqueness of the Trawin Zero Point
master_row.canonical_statement: There exists exactly one point $p$ in the Trawinistic manifold $M$ such that for all geodesics $\gamma$ in $M$, $\lim_{\tau\to\infty}\gamma(\tau)=p$; furthermore, $p$ is the unique fixed point under any symmetry-preserving diffeomorphism $\phi: M \to M$.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a limiting process relating p.
master_row.dictionary: There exists exactly one point (p) in the Trawinistic manifold (M) such that for all geodesics () in (M), (_()=p); furthermore, (p) is the unique fixed point under any symmetry‑preserving diffeomorphism (:M M).
master_row.encyclopedia: The TZP is established as the terminal convergence point of the system. This makes $p$ not merely a coordinate, but the limiting identity-point toward which the primary trajectory collapses. Interpretive role: The zero point becomes the reference from which every later registry object inherits orientation, admissibility, and scale. Without this closure, there is no stable notion of a TRAWIN-normalized manifold, unit, or operator chain.
